sections.description: AI-powered CSS/JS asset scanner for WordPress.
sections.changelog: Version 1.7.80b (beta): the scanner can now read WordPress's declared script dependencies. WordPress knows which scripts each script depends on; the scanner never did, and inferred it from the rendered page — so a script that looked unused could be unloaded even though a script you are keeping declares it as a dependency, breaking the page in exactly the case unloading is meant to be safe (real example: wc-add-to-cart-variation declares wp-util; wp-util measured zero coverage, was unloaded, and broke the variable-product purchase flow on a live store). The plugin now publishes a small JSON dependency island on the page, ONLY on the scanner's own authenticated capture request and never on a normal visitor's page view; it is removed from the page body before the scanner measures it, and capped at 128 KB. This release ships the producer side only — the worker-side guard runs in observation mode and changes no scan result yet. Also: the tech-stack popup no longer interrupts a clean probe (it is now a self-dismissing notification), while the security-stack warning is UNCHANGED and still blocks — if a WAF or security stack is detected, or the probe fails, cannot identify the stack, or the target is not WordPress, you still get the blocking Cancel/Continue prompt so you can bail before any credits are spent. Security: the temporary token authorising the scanner's own requests to your site is now generated with a cryptographically secure random source (128-bit) instead of WordPress's UUID helper.
